description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization formed after the demobilization of the United Self-Defense Forces of Colombia (AUC). It was involved in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and terrorism. The group emerged in 2006 in Norte de Santander and expanded to other departments. It was led by Daniel Rendón Herrera (alias Don Mario) and was largely dismantled by 2014, with remnants joining the Clan del Golfo.

country_links.evidence_quote: Venezuela declaró en su momento a este grupo paramilitar como terrorista desde que empezaron a hostigar sectores fronterizos, además de cometer crímenes hacia la población indígena venezolana.
country_links.context: The Águilas Negras operated in border areas and committed crimes against indigenous people in Venezuela.
